topic: fraction division word problem, reasoning about operations
latex:
An art teacher has $3$ gallons of paint to pour into containers. If each container holds $\frac{3}{4}$ gallon, how many containers can they fill? Complete the sentence below.

To solve, we'll need to $\begin{pmatrix} \text{multiply } 3/4 \text{ by } 3 \\ \text{divide } 3 \text{ by } 3/4 \end{pmatrix}$, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL gallons of paint} \end{pmatrix}$, but we don't know the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL gallons of paint} \end{pmatrix}$.
answer_latex: To solve, we'll need to \textbf{divide 3 by 3/4}, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the \textbf{TOTAL gallons of paint}, but we don't know the \textbf{NUMBER of groups}.
